chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P. No. S-380 of 2025 Date Orders with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objection flag ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 27-10-2025 Mr. Rashid Ali Tunio, Advocate a/w petitioners. Mr. Munwar Ali Abbasi, Assistant A.G. Mr. Zain-ul-Abideen Abbasi, A.P.G for the State. ************** Learned Assistant Advocate General files comments/statements on behalf of respondents No.3 and 4, which are taken on record. Copies thereof have been supplied to the learned counsel for the petitioners. Mr. Farooq Ahmed Gaad, Advocate, files vakalatnama on behalf of respondent No.5, which is also taken on record. Learned counsel for the petitioners contends that respondent No.5 has been harassing and threatening the petitioners on account of a complaint filed against him in the year 2020. Conversely, learned counsel for respondent No.5 submits that the respondent No.5 has not caused any harassment to the petitioners, nor does he intend to do so in the future, as alleged. He further submits that respondent No.5 had moved a complaint through the Prime Minister's Portal, and the instant petition has been filed by the petitioners merely to pressurize him to withdraw said complaint. Learned Assistant Advocate General submits that the official respondents are duty-bound to act strictly in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioners, if and when required. Petitioners’ counsel after going through the comments/statements filed by the official respondents, does not press the petition on merits but reiterates the request for protection. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with a direction to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and provide protection to the petitioners as and when required.

Petitioners’ counsel after going through the comments/statements filed by the official respondents, does not press the petition on merits but reiterates the request for protection. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with a direction to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and provide protection to the petitioners as and when required. As regards the grievances of the petitioners and respondent No.5 against each other, they may approach the competent authority/proper forum for redressal of their grievances, if so advised. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, as well as to the official respondents concerned, for information and necessary compliance. Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
